This is a config-level review only; it is **not** a fresh PK model rerun or formal stability validation.", recipients="")]Yes — I pulled the latest dosing config and summarized it.

**Quick dosing summary for Dr. Martinez**
- **Compound:** NXB-7042
- **Indication:** Pediatric Neuroblastoma (Stage IV)
- **Config version:** v3
- **Last updated:** 2026-03-28

| Patient | Weight | Dose | Calculated dose | Frequency | Next dose | PK clearance |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|---|---:|
| CU-001 | 12.4 kg | 0.15 mg/kg | **1.86 mg** | q3w | 2026-04-14 | 3.2 mL/min |
| CU-002 | 9.1 kg | 0.12 mg/kg | **1.09 mg** | q3w | 2026-04-12 | 2.8 mL/min |
| CU-003 | 15.7 kg | 0.18 mg/kg | **2.83 mg** | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 mL/min |

**Clinical notes**
- **CU-001:** Stable; tumor regression 22% at last scan
- **CU-002:** Responding well; weight gain improving
- **CU-003:** New enrollment; first dose given 2026-03-27

**PK models / stability**
At the **config level**, everything looks internally consistent and I don’t see any obvious red flags in the listed dosing or clearance values.  
That said, this was a **parameter review**, not a fresh PK model rerun, so I can say the current settings look stable on paper but not claim a full revalidation from this check alone.

I also saved this as a report: **“Compassionate-Use Dosing Summary for Dr. Martinez.”**
